findings.title: buildRepositoryStruct defensive null pattern is uniform
findings.description: buildRepositoryStruct applies `?? null` uniformly to all fields. The comment block explains the defensive reasoning against future schema drift on Repository. The new code is internally consistent. Note this was raised as a concern but the patch shows the null handling is already applied to all five fields.

findings.title: skill_version maps skillId which is not a semver string
findings.description: The payload field `skill_version` is sourced from `review.skillId` which holds values like 'code-review@5.0.0'. The BQ column name `skill_version` implies a plain semver string, but the actual value includes the skill name prefix. This semantic mismatch may confuse downstream BQ consumers expecting a version-only value.

findings.title: publishReviewCompleted JSDoc misleads about throws
findings.description: publishReviewCompleted's JSDoc states 'Fire-and-forget: Pub/Sub errors are logged, not re-thrown' but it actually DOES re-throw on PR-fetch errors. Meanwhile publishFinding truly never re-throws. The docstrings are slightly misleading about the actual error contract.

findings.title: Module docstring says never re-thrown but now it is
findings.description: The module-level JSDoc (lines 9-11) states: 'Pub/Sub errors are caught and logged — never re-thrown — so a publish failure never breaks the main request path.' After this PR, publishReviewCompleted re-throws on getPullRequest() failures, violating the stated contract.

findings.title: N+1 Firestore reads: each finding re-fetches same PR
findings.description: publishFinding() calls getPullRequestManager().getPullRequest() on every invocation. In a review with N findings, the same PR document is fetched N times from Firestore. The PR document is immutable during the review window, so a single fetch (either cached or passed as a parameter) would eliminate N-1 redundant reads.

findings.title: Inline repository struct diverges from buildRepositoryStruct
findings.description: buildPrEventPayload() inlines its own repository shape that differs from the shared buildRepositoryStruct() helper: it omits defensive `?? null` on `full_name` and `platform`. A null/undefined repository.fullName or platform will produce undefined (dropped by JSON.stringify) in PR lifecycle events but null in finding/review events — inconsistent BQ inserts across event types.

findings.title: Missing test: getPullRequest throws for review
findings.description: The test suite covers the case where getPullRequest returns null (skips publish), but does not cover the case where getPullRequest throws. Since publishReviewCompleted re-throws on error (unlike publishFinding which catches), this path should be tested to verify the throw propagates correctly.

findings.title: Shallow spread of as const DEFAULT_MODEL is fragile
findings.description: `model: { ...DEFAULT_MODEL }` does a shallow spread of the `as const` object. While safe today (all values are primitives), if DEFAULT_MODEL ever gains a nested object, the spread would share references. A factory function would be more conventional.

findings.title: publishReviewCompleted throws on transient PR fetch
findings.description: publishReviewCompleted re-throws getPullRequest() errors (line 384: `throw err`), permanently losing the review completion event. During a transient Firestore blip, all findings still publish (publishFinding catches and degrades to null structs), but the parent review is dropped — creating orphaned findings in BQ with no review row. publishFinding's graceful-degradation pattern should be mirrored here.
